_comment: Template merge profile for custom Salesforce objects. Copy and customize for your custom objects.
description: Merge profile for [YourCustomObject] - describe the object and its merge requirements

relatedObjects._comment: List all objects that have lookup/master-detail relationships to this object
relatedObjects.description: Related records that should be moved to master record
Tasks related to this custom object (polymorphic WhatId field)

specialCases._comment: Define any object-specific merge requirements or constraints

customValidation.description: Describe any special validation rules for this object
customValidation.cliImplementation: Describe how to validate this in CLI

fieldResolution._comment: Keywords that indicate important fields for smart merge strategy
fieldResolution.description: Master values win unless null (standard runbook rule)

validation._comment: Validation checks to run before merge
validation.description: Add validation checks specific to your object

performance.description: Performance optimizations (leave as-is unless you have specific requirements)

runbookCompliance.soapEquivalent: merge() call with custom object masterRecord
runbookCompliance.cliImplementation: sf data query + sf data update + sf data delete
runbookCompliance.fieldResolutionRule: Master values win unless null (runbook default)
runbookCompliance.relatedRecordHandling: Automatic reparenting via CSV bulk update

notes: This is a template - customize all fields for your specific object
Remove _comment fields before using in production
Test thoroughly in sandbox before production use
Document any object-specific merge requirements in notes
Add all related objects that need to be reparented during merge

howToUse: 1. Copy this file to [objectname]-merge-profile.json (lowercase object name)
2. Update 'object' and 'apiName' to your custom object API name
3. List all related objects in 'relatedObjects' array
4. Add importance keywords relevant to your object fields
5. Define any special validation requirements in 'specialCases'
7. Validate in sandbox before production deployment
exampleUsage: node generic-record-merger.js production a01xxx001 a01xxx002 --strategy favor-master
relatedObjectsDiscovery: Use sf sobject describe CustomObject__c to find childRelationships
Each childRelationship shows: relationshipName, childSObject, field
Add each child object to relatedObjects array with appropriate settings
polymorphicFields: WhoId: Can reference Lead or Contact (for Task/Event)
WhatId: Can reference most standard and custom objects (for Task/Event)
Mark polymorphic: true if the field can reference multiple object types
Polymorphic fields require special handling during reparenting
hierarchyObjects: If your object has self-referential lookup (e.g., Parent__c field):
Enable checkCircularHierarchy validation to prevent infinite loops
